_meta.description: Persistent living knowledge for the Democratic Integrity monitor (WDM). Carries forward the country heatmap classifications, mimicry chain tracking, and active institutional integrity flags across weekly issues.

baseliner_corrections.reason: preserve from current; subagent emitted first-build pattern
normalise to session canonical value (subagent used spec literal)

structural_facts.fact: V-Dem Democracy Report 2026 (10th edition) finds global democracy at 1978 levels. 92 autocracies vs 87 democracies as of end-2025; 44 autocratizing countries; 18 democratizing. Third wave of democratization gains almost fully eradicated.

Freedom House Freedom in the World 2026 ('The Growing Shadow of Autocracy'): global freedom declined for 20th consecutive year in 2025. 54 countries experienced deterioration; 35 improved. Only 21% of world population lives in 'free' countries (down from 46% two decades ago). US score 81/100 (dropped 3 points, cumulative -12 since 2005).

V-Dem 2026 identifies 10 new autocratizers in 2025; 5 are European: Croatia, Italy, Slovakia, Slovenia, UK. USA LDI fell from 0.75 (2024) to 0.57 (2025) — 24% decline in one year, unprecedented in modern history. USA dropped from rank 20 to rank 51 globally on LDI.

Schedule Policy/Career (formerly Schedule F): OPM finalized rule February 5, 2026, effective March 9, 2026. Approximately 50,000 federal employees could be reclassified as effectively at-will, stripped of civil service appeal protections. Implements EO 14171 (January 20, 2025).

Trump v. Barbara: SCOTUS heard oral arguments April 1, 2026 on EO 14160 (birthright citizenship). Majority of justices signaled skepticism toward administration position; decision expected June-July 2026. First potential reinterpretation of 14th Amendment citizenship clause by executive action since Wong Kim Ark (1898).

Hungary electoral system structural fact: 2024 redistricting required Tisza to outperform polls by 3-5 percentage points to win a simple majority. Supermajority threshold is 2/3 of 199 National Assembly seats (133 seats). Electoral system biased toward incumbency through single-member constituency design.

Georgia: Foreign Agents Registration Act (FARA-style) passed April 1, 2025, entered force June 1, 2025. Criminal liability up to 5 years for non-compliance. Requires registration as 'foreign agent' for individuals/organizations receiving foreign funding for broadly-defined 'political activity'.

Georgia: January 2026 law package proposes criminal penalties up to 6-12 years for receiving foreign grants without prior government authorization, 8-year political party membership ban for former employees of foreign-funded organizations, and state control over all foreign-funded civil society assets. Council of Europe Commissioner for Human Rights states amendments conflict with Georgia's international obligations.

Turkey: Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu arrested March 19, 2025 on corruption and terrorism charges; formally jailed March 23. Arrest occurred on day CHP nominated him as presidential candidate. Istanbul University annulled his degree March 18, 2025 — a prerequisite disqualification from presidential candidacy. Over 15 CHP mayors and hundreds of staff subsequently detained.

Turkey: V-Dem LDI 0.12 (2024 data). EU rapporteur November 2025 characterized Turkey as facing 'worst democratic decline in history.' ECHR has previously found Turkey detained Imamoglu predecessor Demirtas and activist Kavala for 'ulterior purpose of stifling pluralism' — both still not released as of 2026.

Mexico: September 2024 constitutional reform eliminated judicial tenure; June 1, 2025 judicial elections filled all 9 Supreme Court seats by popular vote (first such election in world history). Morena-backed candidates swept all 9 SCJN seats. Turnout only 13%. HRW: reform undermines judicial independence. New Supreme Court president Hugo Aguilar inaugurated September 1, 2025.

India: V-Dem 2026 classifies India as electoral autocracy (entry 2017); ranked 105/179 on LDI (down from 100 in 2025). Freedom House 2026: Partly Free, 62/100. March 2026 FCRA amendment tabled in Lok Sabha: creates 'Designated Authority' able to seize assets of NGOs with revoked licenses. 21,933 organizations had FCRA licenses revoked as of March 26, 2026.

South Korea: Constitutional Court unanimously impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol April 4, 2025 for December 3, 2024 martial law declaration. Yoon sentenced to life imprisonment for insurrection February 2026. Lee Jae-myung (Democratic Party) won snap presidential election June 3, 2025 with 49.3% of vote vs 41.3% for PPP's Kim Moon-soo; 80% turnout.

Romania: Constitutional Court annulled November 2024 presidential election (first round won by Calin Georgescu) citing Russian interference evidence (85,000 cyberattacks, 25,000 TikTok accounts). Repeated election May 4/18, 2025. Pro-EU independent Nicusor Dan won 53.60% vs George Simion (AUR far-right) 46.40%. Turnout 64.72% (record). EU Liberties RoL Report 2026: Romania classified 'Stagnator'.

Venezuela: Maduro seized by US military operation in early January 2026. Acting President Delcy Rodriguez assumed office. Amnesty law signed February 19, 2026 (unanimously approved National Assembly), covering political offenses 1999-2026. Foro Penal: 473 political prisoners released as of April 20, 2026; over 500 estimated still detained. Rights groups criticize slow pace and Rodriguez's April 2026 suggestion the release program may end.

Serbia: November 2024 Novi Sad railway station canopy collapse (16 killed) triggered sustained mass protests throughout 2025 — largest in Serbia's history (up to 325,000 in March 2025 Belgrade march). Protests continue through April 2026. Vucic has made no substantive concessions. V-Dem LDI 0.35. Freedom House 2025: Partly Free (56/100). Snap parliamentary elections expected by end-2026.

windowed_events.event: Hungary parliamentary election: Tisza Party (Peter Magyar) won landslide supermajority — 141/199 seats (53.18% of vote). Fidesz-KDNP won only 52 seats (38.61%). Record turnout 79.56%. Orban conceded defeat, ending 16-year rule. Final results confirmed by National Election Office April 18, 2026. Tisza holds supermajority sufficient to amend Fundamental Law.
windowed_events.significance: Critical: Democratic reversal — end of 16-year autocratization episode under Orban. Recovery trajectory begins.

version_history.reason: Election outcome confirmed April 18 2026 by NEO

windowed_events.event: Hungarian President Tamas Sulyok met all three parliamentary party leaders on April 15, 2026 and announced he would nominate Peter Magyar as Prime Minister when the legislature convenes. New National Assembly expected to convene May 9, 2026.
windowed_events.significance: High: Constitutional transfer of power confirmed

windowed_events.event: Benin presidential election: Romuald Wadagni (Finance Minister, ruling coalition, Talon's chosen successor) won 94.05% of vote vs Paul Hounkpe (6%). Turnout 58.75%. Constitutional Court confirmed result April 23, 2026. Main opposition party (Democrats) excluded due to insufficient parliamentary endorsements. Only two ruling-bloc candidates cleared. Observers reported pre-filled ballot boxes but ECOWAS declared aberrations insufficient to undermine credibility.
windowed_events.significance: High: Confirms escalation to RapidDecay — election outcome was foregone conclusion due to structural exclusion of opposition. Wadagni takes office May 24, 2026.

version_history.reason: Election outcome confirmed by Constitutional Court

windowed_events.event: OSCE Moscow Mechanism report on Georgia released March 12, 2026. Invoked by 23 OSCE participating states on January 29, 2026. Rapporteur (Prof. Patrycja Grzebyk) finding: 'marked democratic backsliding has taken place in Georgia.' Pattern of violence reaching threshold of torture; near-total impunity for perpetrators; laws systematically restrict fundamental freedoms; attempt to ban major opposition parties threatens political pluralism.
windowed_events.significance: Critical: Moscow Mechanism invoked (rarely used); 24/57 OSCE states endorsed recommendations; targeted sanctions recommended

windowed_events.event: Georgian Dream ruling party passed sweeping civil society crackdown legislation: criminal penalties up to 12 years for unauthorized foreign grants; 8-year party membership ban for former foreign-funded org employees; government seizure authority over CSO assets. Amnesty International, Amnesty Council of Europe Commissioner condemn as incompatible with international obligations. March 2026: laws passed.
windowed_events.significance: Critical: Most severe civil society legislation in Georgia's post-Soviet history

windowed_events.event: V-Dem Democracy Report 2026 published. USA LDI declined 24% in one year (0.75 to 0.57), faster than Hungary (4 years), Serbia (8 years), India or Turkey (~decade). USA loses liberal democracy status for first time in 50+ years. Legislative Constraints losing 1/3 of value in 2025, reaching lowest in 100+ years. Civil Rights & Equality and Freedom of Expression at lowest in 60 years.
windowed_events.significance: Critical: Anchors severity_score and vdemLDI for USA entries

windowed_events.event: Turkish court issued second arrest order for Imamoglu on 'political espionage' charges (October 27, 2025). State simultaneously seized Tele1 TV channel (critical of government). By October 2025: 15+ CHP mayors, hundreds of staff detained. Imamoglu faces 2,430-year sentence request in mass corruption trial (March 9, 2026 hearing).
windowed_events.significance: High: Systematic opposition elimination — escalation from single arrest to party-wide judicial crackdown

windowed_events.event: Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to life imprisonment for insurrection by Seoul Central Court, February 2026. Assessment: 'democratic erosion not inevitable' — constitutional resilience demonstrated. Lee Jae-myung government facing challenges: PPPK fragmentation, economic headwinds, constitutional reform deferred. South Korea V-Dem LDI 0.63 (V-Dem v16).
windowed_events.significance: High: Landmark accountability — rare democratic resilience signal in global backsliding environment

windowed_events.event: Romania presidential election (repeated after November 2024 annulment): Nicusor Dan (pro-EU independent) won 53.60% vs George Simion (AUR far-right) 46.40%. Record 11 million voters (64.72% turnout). Simion initially refused to concede. Constitutional Court barred Georgescu from standing. EU Liberties RoL 2026 classifies Romania as 'Stagnator' (functioning framework, persistent shortcomings).
windowed_events.significance: High: Democratic resilience — defeated hard-right challenge despite Russian interference campaign

windowed_events.event: Mexico held world's first nationwide judicial election. Morena-backed candidates swept all 9 SCJN Supreme Court seats (13% voter turnout). Six of nine justices took office September 1, 2025. Critics: candidates pre-screened by Morena-controlled committees; judicial independence eliminated. Sheinbaum attended SCJN inauguration — critics cite interference.
windowed_events.significance: High: Structural elimination of judicial independence via constitutional reform

windowed_events.event: India tabled FCRA Amendment 2026 in Lok Sabha (March 25, 2026): creates 'Designated Authority' to seize NGO assets on license cancellation; extends enforcement reach. As of 26 March 2026: 21,933 organizations had FCRA licenses revoked. Parliamentary consideration deferred April 2026 (opposition, religious groups, Kerala elections). April 2026 deferral does not eliminate threat.
windowed_events.significance: High: Escalation of civil society crackdown — FATF 2024 noted FCRA risks misuse for legitimate CSO restriction

version_history.reason: ICNL Civic Freedom Monitor + Amnesty primary documentation

windowed_events.event: Venezuela amnesty law signed by acting President Delcy Rodriguez (February 19, 2026). Covers political offenses 1999-2026. Excludes exile opposition (initially), serious crimes. Foro Penal: 473 confirmed releases as of April 20, 2026 vs 1,557 applications. Rodriguez April 2026 suggested program conclusion — condemned by Foro Penal and Provea as unconstitutional overreach. Washington's 3-stage stabilization plan (stabilization → recovery → transition) without timeline.
windowed_events.significance: High: Transition credibility test — gap between official claims and Foro Penal data widens confidence erosion

version_history.reason: BBC April 25 2026 report on Foro Penal figures

windowed_events.event: Serbia: First anniversary of Novi Sad station collapse. Tens of thousands marched to Novi Sad. Protests sustained 16+ months without political breakthrough. June 28, 2025 Slavija Square protest (140,000 participants) ended in police violence; 4 protesters hospitalized. Council of Europe Commissioner and UN OHCHR expressed concern about excessive force. Vucic rejected snap elections but hinted at compromise. Vucic reaches 2-term limit in 2027 (cannot run again).
windowed_events.significance: High: Largest sustained protest movement in Serbian history; democratic stalemate persists

institutional_integrity_active_flags.signal: DNI Tulsi Gabbard's 2026 Worldwide Threats Hearing (March 19) omitted foreign election interference threats — for the first time in nearly a decade. Defense One (March 18): 'For the first time in near

Reuters analysis (March 7): 97% of Trump's 31 Supreme Court emergency requests argue judicial interference with presidential power — a systematic campaign to reframe judicial review as illegitimate. Schedule Policy/Career (March 9, 2026) strips 50,000 federal employees of civil service protections. Trump v. Barbara SCOTUS hearing April 1, 2026 on birthright citizenship; decision June-July 2026.

New March 2026 law: 8-year ban on political party membership for anyone previously employed by a foreign-funded organisation. This structurally excludes the entire civil society professional class from political participation. OSCE Moscow Mechanism rapporteur confirms 'marked democratic backsliding' and torture-level treatment of detainees with near-total impunity.

April 12, 2026 election: Wadagni won 94% with only token opposition (Democrats excluded). Constitutional Court confirmed April 23. ECOWAS reported ballot irregularities (pre-filled boxes) but declared insufficient to invalidate. Escalation trigger crossed. Wadagni inaugurated May 24, 2026.

Electoral redistricting (2024) engineered to require Tisza to outperform polls by 3–5 points for a parliamentary majority — a structural advantage that operated until April 12, 2026 election when Tisza overcame the structural disadvantage with a supermajority (141/199 seats). ODIHR deployed for April 12 election. Flag status: RESOLVED — April 12 election confirmed democratic transition.
institutional_integrity_active_flags.resolution_note: Tisza supermajority overcame structural gerrymandering. Orban conceded. Flag downgraded from Active to Resolved.

institutional_integrity_active_flags.signal: GTI 2026: Colombia re-entered top 10 terrorism-impacted countries — 70% increase in deaths, 47% increase in attacks. IEP identifies state inability to manage cross-border counterinsurgency as the structural driver.

SCOTUS heard oral arguments April 1 in Trump v. Barbara — whether EO 14160 can redefine '14th Amendment subject to jurisdiction' by executive action alone, stripping birthright citizenship from children of undocumented and temporary-visa parents. Decision expected June/July 2026. If upheld, represents the first major reinterpretation of a constitutional citizenship clause by executive fiat, without amendment or legislation, since Wong Kim Ark (1898). SCOTUS majority signaled skepticism (NPR, April 1, 2026).
institutional_integrity_active_flags.lead_time: Decision June/July 2026 — leading indicator of constitutional resilience or erosion

Evaluation committees controlled by Morena pre-screened candidates. HRW: reform 'undermines judicial independence.' Sheinbaum attended SCJN inauguration; critics cite executive interference. February 2026: further electoral reform proposed.

16+ month protest movement (Nov 2024 to present) — largest in Serbia history. June 2025 police violence against protesters (CoE/UN condemnation). Vucic has made no substantive concessions. Movement shifting from civic to political actor (announced electoral list). Snap parliamentary elections expected end-2026. Vucic cannot run in 2027 presidential election (2-term limit).

calibration_log.summary: vdemLDI anchors + ERT flags for 15 countries; USA electoralWatch HIGH_RISK; 4 Watchlist additions

Full DR re-verification pass. Key updates: Hungary democratic reversal (April 12 election), Benin election confirmed, Georgia OSCE Moscow Mechanism primary source verified, Turkey promoted to RapidDecay, V-Dem 2026 and Freedom House 2026 structural facts added, Venezuela Foro Penal figures updated, Serbia protest escalation documented, Mexico judicial elections documented, India FCRA 2026 documented, South Korea Yoon sentencing and Lee election documented.

gaps_register.description: OSCE/ODIHR full election observation report for Hungary April 12, 2026 election not yet published (typically released 1-2 months post-election). When published, should populate windowed_events and update Hungary Recovery entry with ODIHR assessment.

Turkey Article 46 ECHR proceedings status not confirmed in this pass. Demirtas and Kavala still detained against ECHR orders — enforcement mechanism status unclear as of April 2026. Should verify whether Council of Europe has moved to infringement proceedings.

Georgia: status of attempt to ban opposition parties (Georgian Dream has filed applications to ban United National Movement and other parties) — outcome as of April 2026 not confirmed. OSCE Moscow Mechanism report flagged this as threat to political pluralism.

Venezuela: Washington's 3-stage stabilization plan lacks timeline. No update on electoral roadmap beyond 'transition roadmap announced'. Missing: whether elections have been scheduled, what electoral framework has been proposed, and whether Machado or other exiled opposition will be permitted to run.

Mexico February 2026 electoral reform proposal (Sheinbaum): eliminates 200 proportional-representation congressional seats, reduces Senate from 128 to 96, cuts electoral spending 25%, bans nepotism and consecutive lawmaker reelection. Bill introduced February 2026 — status (passed/pending) not confirmed in this pass.

EU Article 7 proceedings against Hungary: pre-Article 7 proceedings were active under Orban. With Tisza election victory, formal status of EU Article 7 probe against Hungary as of April 2026 not confirmed — may be suspended or discontinued under new government.

Serbia snap elections: Vucic has accepted elections by end-2026 in principle. Specific date not confirmed as of April 28, 2026. Student movement's electoral list not yet published. V-Dem Serbia LDI update from v16 not confirmed (currently using v15 figure of 0.35).
